summary: Decompiled a slice of Bowser/Koopa side-B logic in `ftKp_SpecialS`, raising the file report to 14.32% matched over 10.78 KB. The PR added C implementations for several SpecialS animation and IASA callbacks, introduced a Koopa motion-vars view for side-B state at fp+2340..fp+234c, refreshed the matching assembly formatting and sdata2 object declarations, and marked related scratch entries as OK. There were no review comments in the provided slice.

key_files.role: Added matched C for selected Koopa SpecialS callbacks and helper inlines.
Added `ftKoopa_SpecialSVars` inside `ftKoopa_MotionVars` for side-B state fields.
Removed/reformatted matched assembly ranges and converted local assembly metadata to modern `.fn`/`.endfn` and `.obj` style.
Updated many Koopa scratch entries around 0x80132A84-0x80133D20 from percent statuses to `OK`.

what_changed: Added includes and C bodies to previously empty `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKoopa/ftKp_SpecialS.c`.

Matched `ftKp_SpecialAirSHit_Anim`, including cmd-var collision handling via `ftColl_8007ABD0`, frame-end checks with `ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining`, transition to continued hit states based on `b_held`, and victim cleanup through `ftCo_800BCAF4`.

Added shared helper `doEndFAnim` for ground and air forward end animations; it handles throw-B4 direction reversal, victim release/capture-damage handoff through `ftCommon_8007E2F4`, `ftCo_800DE2A8`, and `ftCo_800DE7C0`, then calls either `ft_8008A2BC` or `ftCo_800CC730` when animation frames end.
Added shared helper `doEndBAnim` for backward end animations; it calls `ftKp_SpecialS_80132E30` and then dispatches to ground or air end callbacks when animation completes.
Added `ftKoopa_SpecialS_ChangeAction`, selecting forward/backward Koopa and victim motion states from `fp->mv.kp.specials.facing_dir` and using `ftCo_800BCDE0` for the victim thrown-state change.

Matched `ftKp_SpecialSHit_IASA` and `ftKp_SpecialAirSHit_IASA`, including B-button held tracking, stick threshold comparisons against `ftKoopaAttributes::x30`, calculation of side-B facing direction as `fp->facing_dir * facing_dir`, and transition to ground/air forward/backward end states.
Added `ftKoopa_SpecialSVars` fields: `b_held` at fp+2340, `x4` at fp+2344, `facing_dir` at fp+2348, and `xC` at fp+234c, with a todo noting possible overlap with `ftKoopa_State1Vars`.

Updated assembly file metadata from older `.section .text`/`.global` labels to `.file`, `.text`, `.balign 4`, `.fn`, `.endfn`, and `.obj`/`.endobj` forms, while preserving remaining unmatched assembly.
Converted many scratch entries in `config/GALE01/scratches.txt` from explicit percent scores to `OK`, reflecting resolved or accepted matches in this range.
smart_moves: Factored duplicated ground/air behavior into callback-parameterized helpers (`doEndFAnim`, `doEndBAnim`) so the same logic can match both ground and aerial variants while only varying the final action callback.

Used explicit motion-state enum names such as `ftKp_MS_SpecialSEndF`, `ftKp_MS_SpecialAirSEndB`, `ftCo_MS_ThrownKoopaF`, and `ftCo_MS_ThrownKoopaAirB`, making the side-B throw flow searchable and understandable instead of leaving raw IDs.
Modeled fp+2340..fp+234c as a named Koopa side-B motion-vars struct, allowing matched C to express persistent action state (`b_held`, `facing_dir`, etc.) instead of opaque offset access.
Left TODOs where evidence was not strong enough: `ftKoopa_SpecialSVars` may be a reused state struct, and the IASA bodies likely share an inline but were not forced into one prematurely.
Used stack padding macros (`PAD_STACK`, `FORCE_PAD_STACK`) and local declaration ordering notes to preserve codegen while keeping the C readable.
Encoded the forward/back side-B transition as a small helper taking Koopa/victim motion-state pairs, which keeps the ground and air IASA functions structurally parallel.
decomp_lessons: For fighter action callbacks with ground/air twins, parameterize the differing terminal callbacks or motion states rather than duplicating large bodies; this can preserve matching while making intent clearer.
When motion vars at `fp->mv.<fighter>` become understandable, add a narrow struct overlay with offset comments. This improves future matching and prevents repeated magic offsets.
If a likely shared inline does not yet match cleanly, document it with a TODO instead of over-abstracting and risking worse codegen.
Declaration order can be matching-sensitive; the diff explicitly notes that `da` could not move below `fp` in the IASA functions.
Stick-threshold IASA logic can require comparing both previous and current stick x-values (`lstick1.x` and `lstick.x`) against fighter attributes to detect directional input changes.
For side-B/throw interactions, model both the attacker motion state and victim thrown motion state together so direction-specific transitions stay synchronized.
Scratch cleanup to `OK` is useful after landing matched source, but it should stay tied to the exact address range changed to avoid losing provenance.
naming_conventions: `ftKp_` prefix is used for Koopa/Bowser functions, matching existing fighter module conventions.
Action callback suffixes follow fighter callback names: `_Anim` for animation callbacks and `_IASA` for interruptibility callbacks.
Ground/air variants are distinguished with `SpecialS...` versus `SpecialAirS...` in function and motion-state names.
Direction variants use `EndF` and `EndB` suffixes for forward/backward side-B endings.
Motion-state enum names use `ftKp_MS_...` for Koopa and `ftCo_MS_...` for common victim states.
Temporary matching helpers were named descriptively but locally (`doEndFAnim`, `doEndBAnim`), while uncertain recovered names retained TODOs.
Motion-vars fields were given semantic names when known (`b_held`, `facing_dir`) and placeholder names when uncertain (`x4`, `xC`).
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(4 * 2)`, `FORCE_PAD_STACK(4 * 2)`, and `PAD_STACK(4 * 4)` to satisfy stack-frame layout requirements.
Used inline helpers (`inlineA0`, `inlineA1`, `ftKoopa_SpecialS_ChangeAction`) to reproduce repeated code sequences and likely original inlining behavior.
Preserved matching-sensitive local ordering in IASA functions, specifically keeping `ftKoopaAttributes* da` placement constrained by the note that it cannot move below `fp`.
Used callback arguments (`HSD_GObjEvent cb`) to share logic while still emitting the distinct calls to `ft_8008A2BC` and `ftCo_800CC730` seen in ground versus air assembly.
Assembly was normalized to modern macro style with `.fn`/`.endfn` function blocks and `.obj`/`.endobj` data blocks.
Remaining assembly literals were normalized to hex/immediate forms such as `0x0`, `0x1`, and bit positions in `rlwimi`/`extrwi`, aiding diff readability.
Small-data constants in the assembly file were rewritten as object declarations for labels such as `ftKp_Init_804D9AD0`, `ftKp_Init_804D9AD8`, and related floats/doubles.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or refine `ftKoopa_SpecialSVars`, especially placeholder fields `x4` and `xC`.
Search for future work that resolves the TODO shared inline between `ftKp_SpecialSHit_IASA` and `ftKp_SpecialAirSHit_IASA`.
Look for PRs matching the remaining unmatched functions in `ftKp_SpecialS.c` after address 0x80133D20.
Compare other fighter throw/capture special moves for the same `ftCo_800DE2A8` and `ftCo_800DE7C0` release pattern.
Search for `ftCheckThrowB4` usages to confirm naming and semantics around reversing `facing_dir` during throw end animations.
